title: Cluster Metrics Management
description:
The ClusterMetrics.java file provides a class to encapsulate and manage cluster status metrics. It maintains various attributes representing the current state of a MapReduce cluster, including running tasks, occupied slots, job submissions, and tracker counts. The class implements the Writable interface for serialization, allowing cluster metrics to be stored and retrieved efficiently. It doesn't take any inputs but outputs cluster status via getter methods. Dependencies include Hadoop's DataInput, DataOutput, IOException, and Writable interfaces, along with Hadoop's classification and mapreduce packages. This class plays a crucial role in monitoring and managing the health and performance of a MapReduce cluster.

TITLE: Job Queue Access Control Lists
DESCRIPTION: The QueueAclsInfo.java class encapsulates Access Control Lists (ACLs) for specific job queues in Apache Hadoop MapReduce. It implements the Writable interface for serialization, storing the queue name and allowed operations. The class provides constructors, getters, setters, and methods for reading from and writing to data streams. It depends on Hadoop's DataInput, DataOutput, IOException, Text, Writable, and WritableUtils classes, along with standard Java classes. This class is essential for managing and enforcing access control policies in Hadoop MapReduce job queues.

TITLE: TaskTracker Information Representation
DESCRIPTION: The TaskTrackerInfo.java class represents information about a TaskTracker in Apache Hadoop MapReduce. It implements the Writable interface for serialization, maintaining fields like name, blacklist status, reason for blacklist, and blacklist report. The class provides constructors, getters, and methods for reading from and writing to data streams. It depends on Hadoop's DataInput, DataOutput, IOException, and Text classes, along with the Writable interface. This class is vital for storing and retrieving details about TaskTrackers, aiding in cluster management and monitoring.

TITLE: ClientProtocol for JobTracker Communication
DESCRIPTION: The ClientProtocol.java interface defines the communication protocol between JobClient and JobTracker in Hadoop MapReduce. It facilitates job submission, status monitoring, and resource management, with methods for job submission, status retrieval, job killing, priority setting, and cluster status inquiry. It also handles delegation tokens and retrieves system directory paths. The interface depends on various Hadoop and Java classes, establishing a standardized communication protocol between JobClient and JobTracker. This interface is pivotal for seamless interaction and management within the Hadoop MapReduce framework.

TITLE: MapReduce Delegation Token Management
DESCRIPTION: The 'security/token/delegation' directory manages MapReduce-specific delegation tokens. It includes classes like DelegationTokenIdentifier, DelegationTokenSecretManager, and DelegationTokenSelector, ensuring secure job submission and tracking via the Delegate pattern. These components leverage Hadoop security and token delegation classes, significantly enhancing MapReduce security.

TITLE: Log Parameters Management in Hadoop MapReduce v2
DESCRIPTION: The LogParams.java file in the Hadoop MapReduce v2 directory (v2) manages log parameters, storing job identifiers like container ID, application ID, node ID, and owner. It uses private instance variables and getter/setter methods, contributing to the logging and monitoring capabilities of the Hadoop MapReduce v2 framework. This class collaborates with other components for managing logs or job-related information.
